action.reasoning: Observation: stronger augmentation + flip TTA improved hidden-test balanced accuracy from 0.522 to 0.564, but training took ~15.7 minutes and class-wise recall is still very weak for empty and squirrel, suggesting persistent background/domain overfitting and some mismatch between high source-val BA (0.826) and target performance. Analysis: the next best incremental change is to explicitly use domain labels for domain generalization rather than only stronger augmentation. A lightweight domain-adversarial head on top of the CLIP features can encourage location-invariant representations with minimal code change and no inference cost. To avoid overcommitting time, train for a moderate 5 epochs first, keep class balancing and augmentation, and preserve the same prediction path. Decision: add optional DANN training using provided domain_labels.txt, save a new checkpoint, run a quick local smoke test on val, and submit eval.
action.command: python train.py --epochs 5 --checkpoint_path checkpoints/dann.pt && python predict.py --data_path /data --checkpoint checkpoints/dann.pt --output smoke_val.json
